text.tspan: GEOHASH: encode 2D coordinates as a 1D string where
EXAMPLE: 'where are restaurants within 1 km of (37.7749, -122.4194)?'
1. Geohash the query point at appropriate precision (e.g. 5 chars = '9q8yy')
2. SCAN your restaurants table for geohash LIKE '9q8yy%'
3. Also check 8 NEIGHBOR cells (boundary case: query is near edge of cell)
4. Filter results by actual euclidean distance
prefix 3 chars (9q8): ~150 km u2014 large region
prefix 5 chars (9q8yy): ~5 km u2014 city neighborhood
prefix 7 chars (9q8yyk1): ~150 m u2014 a few blocks
u2022 H3 (Uber) u2014 hexagonal grid; uniform cell distances; better than square grids for proximity
u2022 S2 (Google) u2014 spherical projection; rich query operations; used in Google Maps, MongoDB, CockroachDB
u2022 Quadtree u2014 recursive 2D subdivision; classic data structure
u2022 R-tree u2014 indexing for rectangles; used in PostGIS, MongoDB geo-index
u2022 KD-tree u2014 multidimensional partitioning; in-memory only typically
